life-better-today.label: Life 100 Years Ago
life-better-today.question: 'Life is better today than it was 100 years ago.' Do you agree?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Life today is significantly better than it was a century ago.
sections.idea: I will argue this with reference to
bullets: advances in healthcare
entertainment options
technology and communication

sections.title: Healthcare
sections.phrase: One of the most convincing arguments is that healthcare has transformed the quality of life.
sections.idea: Healthcare plays an essential role because
bullets: diseases are now treated effectively
vaccines eliminated polio and smallpox
life expectancy has increased dramatically

sections.title: Entertainment
sections.phrase: Furthermore, the range of entertainment available today is incomparable to the past.
sections.idea: Entertainment options have expanded enormously —
bullets: streaming platforms offer unlimited access
people enjoy any content anytime from home
there are far more ways to relax and unwind

sections.title: Technology
sections.phrase: Finally, technology has fundamentally transformed the way we communicate.
sections.idea: Technology transformed communication by enabling
bullets: video calls connecting families across continents
instant messaging replacing slow letters
access to information at any moment

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: In conclusion, life today is undeniably better than it was 100 years ago.
sections.idea: Progress in health, entertainment, and technology represents
bullets: remarkable improvements for all
a higher quality of life overall

work-money-enjoyment.label: Money vs Enjoyment
work-money-enjoyment.question: Is it better to earn a lot of money or to enjoy your job?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Work is an essential part of life, yet people disagree about what matters most in a career.
sections.idea: This essay will analyse the benefits of
bullets: financial security from a high salary
job satisfaction and personal fulfilment
mental health and long-term wellbeing

sections.title: High Salary
sections.phrase: The main advantage of a high salary is that it provides genuine financial security.
sections.idea: Financial security matters because it allows people to
bullets: afford comfortable lifestyles and support families
access quality healthcare and education
feel stable — we spend a third of our lives working

sections.title: Job Satisfaction
sections.phrase: However, focusing only on money can seriously damage a person's happiness.
sections.idea: Job satisfaction matters greatly since
bullets: boring or stressful jobs lead to unhappiness
motivation disappears without meaning
wellbeing suffers regardless of salary

sections.title: Mental Health
sections.phrase: Moreover, unfulfilling work is a significant cause of burnout, anxiety, and depression.
sections.idea: Mental health is directly affected by job satisfaction —
bullets: meaningful work protects overall wellbeing
unhappy workers underperform and feel exhausted
career choice affects life quality profoundly

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: To sum up, both financial reward and job satisfaction have clear advantages.
sections.idea: The ideal career combines
bullets: a sense of purpose and meaning
a reasonable and sustainable income

endangered-animals.label: Saving Animals
endangered-animals.question: 'We should do everything we can to save animals which are in danger of disappearing.' Do you agree?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Protecting endangered animals has become one of the most urgent environmental issues of our time.
sections.idea: I strongly believe we should do everything possible to
bullets: save species for future generations
protect biodiversity and ecosystems
fulfil our moral responsibility to nature

sections.title: Animals in Danger
sections.phrase: Many magnificent species are currently in danger of extinction.
sections.idea: Tigers, rhinos, and elephants face serious threats from
bullets: illegal hunting and poaching
habitat destruction caused by humans
the permanent and irreversible loss of wildlife

sections.title: Reasons to Protect
sections.phrase: There are compelling reasons why every species deserves protection.
sections.idea: Every animal plays a vital role because
bullets: bees pollinate the crops we depend on for food
losing one species affects many others
our own survival is ultimately threatened too

sections.title: Ecosystem Balance
sections.phrase: Furthermore, ecosystem balance depends entirely on biodiversity.
sections.idea: Each animal contributes to the food chain —
bullets: wolves control deer populations and protect forests
balanced ecosystems maintain environmental stability
human interference has already caused dangerous imbalances

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: In conclusion, endangered animals must be protected without hesitation.
sections.idea: Conservation benefits both nature and humanity by
bullets: preserving the planet's biological heritage
ensuring environmental stability for future generations

friends-vs-family.label: Friends vs Family
friends-vs-family.question: Which is more important – friends or family?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Relationships with friends and family are both essential to human happiness.
sections.idea: This essay will consider the benefits of both by examining
bullets: who we enjoy ourselves with
who supports us in difficult times
how importance shifts across life stages

sections.title: Friends & Enjoyment
sections.phrase: The main advantage of friendships is the enjoyment and shared interests they provide.
sections.idea: Friends are chosen because they share our
bullets: hobbies, values, and sense of humour
social world, especially during teenage years
positive memories and daily happiness

sections.title: Family & Support
sections.phrase: However, family typically provides a stronger and more reliable form of support.
sections.idea: Family offers unconditional support that
bullets: remains available during serious crises
friends may be unwilling or unable to provide
comes without conditions or judgement

sections.title: Life Stages
sections.phrase: Interestingly, the relative importance of each relationship changes throughout life.
sections.idea: Different relationships matter at different stages —
bullets: teenagers prioritise friends for identity and fun
adults increasingly value family for stability
both serve important purposes throughout our lives

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: To sum up, both friends and family are extremely valuable in different ways.
sections.idea: While family tends to be more reliable in a crisis,
bullets: both types of relationship contribute to a fulfilling life
neither should be neglected or taken for granted

environment-pollution.label: Pollution Solutions
environment-pollution.question: Do you think the world's pollution problems can be solved?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Pollution has become one of the most serious threats to health and the natural environment.
sections.idea: This essay will examine problems and solutions related to
bullets: harmful emissions from transport
contamination of rivers and seas
the potential of renewable energy

sections.title: Transport
sections.phrase: Transport is responsible for a significant proportion of global air pollution.
sections.idea: Millions of vehicles produce harmful emissions that cause
bullets: dangerous smog in major cities worldwide
serious respiratory diseases in urban populations
long-term damage to air quality and climate

sections.title: Water Pollution
sections.phrase: Rivers and seas are being contaminated at an alarming rate.
sections.idea: Factories release toxic chemicals and plastic waste that
bullets: kill marine animals and destroy ecosystems
enter the food chain and affect human health
take hundreds of years to break down

sections.title: Renewable Energy
sections.phrase: Fortunately, renewable energy offers a promising solution to pollution.
sections.idea: Solar and wind power produce no emissions and
bullets: countries investing in them show improved air quality
can dramatically reduce dependence on fossil fuels
create jobs while protecting the environment

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: In conclusion, pollution can be addressed effectively with the right commitment.
sections.idea: Cleaner transport, stricter regulations, and renewables will
bullets: significantly reduce harmful emissions over time
protect the planet for future generations

technology-social-interaction.label: Technology & Socialising
technology-social-interaction.question: Is technology making us less sociable?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Technology has transformed the way we communicate, with both positive and negative effects.
sections.idea: This essay will analyse arguments on both sides, focusing on
bullets: online vs face-to-face communication
the effects of social media on behaviour
long-distance relationships and connectivity

sections.title: Global Connection
sections.phrase: One major advantage of technology is that it connects people instantly across vast distances.
sections.idea: Social media allows worldwide communication by enabling
bullets: families to video call relatives living abroad
friendships to be maintained across borders
people to stay connected in ways previously impossible

sections.title: Less Face-to-Face
sections.phrase: However, a key disadvantage is that technology reduces meaningful face-to-face interaction.
sections.idea: People stare at screens instead of talking, which means
bullets: families sit in restaurants without conversing
genuine social skills may gradually decline
real human connection is increasingly replaced

sections.title: Long-Distance
sections.phrase: On balance, technology has clearly benefited long-distance relationships.
sections.idea: Regular video calls and messaging maintain bonds that
bullets: letters and phone calls never could sustain
keep couples and friends emotionally close
turn distance into something manageable

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: In conclusion, technology has both benefits and drawbacks for social interaction.
sections.idea: Used responsibly, it enhances communication and
bullets: brings people together across the world
should be balanced with meaningful in-person contact

gap-year.label: Gap Year
gap-year.question: Is taking a gap year before university a good idea?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Gap years have become increasingly popular among students finishing secondary school.
sections.idea: This essay will analyse both advantages and disadvantages, including
bullets: the life experience gained through travel
the risk of losing academic momentum
the financial considerations involved

sections.title: Life Experience
sections.phrase: The main advantage of a gap year is the invaluable life experience it provides.
sections.idea: Travelling and volunteering develop maturity because students
bullets: learn to solve problems independently
gain confidence and cultural awareness
often perform better when they return to study

sections.title: Losing Momentum
sections.phrase: However, a key disadvantage is the risk of losing academic momentum.
sections.idea: Study habits can deteriorate after a year away —
bullets: some students struggle to return to education
motivation and focus may significantly decline
returning to deadlines and exams feels difficult

sections.title: Financial Factors
sections.phrase: Financial considerations are also significant and should not be overlooked.
sections.idea: Travelling requires substantial savings, meaning
bullets: expenses can reach thousands of pounds
many students work during their gap year to fund university
financial planning is essential for a successful year

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: To sum up, a gap year offers both real benefits and genuine risks.
sections.idea: It is most likely to benefit motivated students who
bullets: plan carefully and set clear goals
use the time productively rather than drifting

online-shopping.label: Online Shopping
online-shopping.question: Is online shopping better than shopping in traditional stores?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Online shopping has grown enormously, transforming the way people buy goods.
sections.idea: This essay will analyse both the benefits and drawbacks, focusing on
bullets: the unmatched convenience it offers
its damaging impact on local businesses
the inability to try products before buying

sections.title: Convenience
sections.phrase: The main advantage of online shopping is its unmatched convenience.
sections.idea: Shoppers can browse and purchase anytime, meaning
bullets: busy people order goods late at night from home
prices are often significantly lower online
delivery arrives directly to the door

sections.title: Local Businesses
sections.phrase: A significant disadvantage, however, is the damage online shopping causes to local businesses.
sections.idea: Independent shops lose customers to online giants and
bullets: many high street stores have been forced to close
town centres are losing their character and vitality
local jobs and communities suffer as a result

sections.title: Trying Products
sections.phrase: Furthermore, shoppers cannot try products before buying online.
sections.idea: This causes inconvenience because
bullets: clothes often fit differently than expected
returns waste time and create frustration
quality cannot be properly assessed from photographs

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: In conclusion, both online and traditional shopping have clear advantages.
sections.idea: Combining the convenience of online shopping with
bullets: the local and tactile experience of physical stores
would represent the most balanced and ideal approach

childhood-obesity.label: Childhood Obesity
childhood-obesity.question: What can be done to reduce childhood obesity?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Childhood obesity has become an increasingly serious health problem in many countries.
sections.idea: This essay will identify the main causes and propose solutions related to
bullets: unhealthy eating habits
a lack of regular exercise
the role of school education

sections.title: Unhealthy Eating
sections.phrase: Unhealthy eating is one of the most significant causes of childhood obesity.
sections.idea: Excessive consumption of sugary snacks and fast food means
bullets: many children consume far more calories than they need
nutritional imbalances develop from an early age
poor dietary habits formed early are difficult to change

sections.title: Lack of Exercise
sections.phrase: A lack of physical exercise also contributes significantly to the problem.
sections.idea: Many children prefer video games and screens to sport, which means
bullets: fitness levels have declined sharply in recent years
calories consumed are not being burned
sedentary habits develop early and persist into adulthood

sections.title: School Education
sections.phrase: School-based education represents one of the most effective solutions available.
sections.idea: Teaching nutrition and providing healthy meals would
bullets: encourage better dietary choices from a young age
banning vending machines would reduce junk food access
increasing PE lessons would improve fitness levels

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: In conclusion, childhood obesity can be effectively addressed through a combination of measures.
sections.idea: Healthier diets, regular exercise, and school programmes
bullets: together represent the most realistic path forward
could significantly reduce obesity rates over time

social-media-addiction.label: Social Media Addiction
social-media-addiction.question: What are the causes and effects of social media addiction?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Social media addiction has become a significant concern, particularly among young people.
sections.idea: This essay will examine both causes and effects, focusing on
bullets: the role of constant notifications
the impact of social comparison
the serious consequences for mental health

sections.title: Constant Notifications
sections.phrase: One of the main causes of social media addiction is the use of constant notifications.
sections.idea: Platforms are deliberately designed to send endless alerts because
bullets: each notification triggers a dopamine response
users feel compelled to check their phones constantly
the cycle of checking and rewarding is highly addictive

sections.title: Social Comparison
sections.phrase: Another powerful cause is the culture of social comparison that social media promotes.
sections.idea: Teenagers compare themselves to influencers and peers, which leads to
bullets: deep feelings of inadequacy and low self-worth
unrealistic expectations of appearance and lifestyle
anxiety about not matching what others appear to have

sections.title: Mental Health Effects
sections.phrase: The effects of social media addiction on mental health are deeply serious.
sections.idea: Constant comparison and screen dependency damages wellbeing by
bullets: causing anxiety, depression, and chronic loneliness
affecting sleep quality and academic performance
reducing real-world social confidence and skills

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: In conclusion, social media addiction is driven by design and causes serious harm.
sections.idea: Understanding these causes is essential so that
bullets: users can recognise and manage addictive patterns
platforms and parents can take responsible protective action

family-size.label: Large vs Small Family
family-size.question: Is it better to be part of a large family or a small family?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Family structure can have a profound effect on a person's development and life experiences.
sections.idea: This essay will analyse the advantages and disadvantages of both, considering
bullets: what children learn in large families
the financial pressures larger families face
the individual attention available in smaller families

sections.title: Learning in Large Families
sections.phrase: One major advantage of large families is the opportunity to develop important social skills.
sections.idea: Growing up with siblings teaches children to
bullets: share, cooperate, and develop patience
support one another through challenges
become less selfish and more sociable overall

sections.title: Financial Pressure
sections.phrase: However, a significant disadvantage is the financial pressure that large families face.
sections.idea: Limited resources shared among more children means
bullets: families experience greater financial stress
opportunities such as travel or private tuition may be limited
quality of life can be affected for everyone

sections.title: Attention in Small Families
sections.phrase: Small families, by contrast, are able to provide far more individual attention.
sections.idea: Parents have more time for each child, which means
bullets: children receive more help with homework and problems
emotional and educational needs are met more easily
parent-child bonds tend to be stronger and deeper

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: To sum up, both large and small families have their own distinct benefits and challenges.
sections.idea: The best family size ultimately depends on
bullets: individual circumstances and available resources
the values and priorities of the parents involved

work-from-home.label: Working from Home
work-from-home.question: Do you agree that working from home is better than working in an office?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Working from home has become increasingly common, sparking debate about its advantages and disadvantages.
sections.idea: This essay will examine arguments on both sides, focusing on
bullets: the time saved by avoiding a commute
the comfort and flexibility of home working
the loss of social interaction with colleagues

sections.title: Time Saving
sections.phrase: A strong argument in favour of remote work is the significant time saved by not commuting.
sections.idea: Eliminating the daily journey to the office means
bullets: workers can save up to two hours every day
stress and fatigue are noticeably reduced
that time can be invested in health, family, or productivity

sections.title: Comfort
sections.phrase: Working from home also offers greater personal comfort and flexibility.
sections.idea: Employees can create personalised workspaces where they
bullets: control temperature, lighting, and noise levels
experience improved productivity and satisfaction
maintain a better balance between work and personal life

sections.title: Social Interaction
sections.phrase: On the other hand, remote work can significantly reduce social interaction.
sections.idea: Isolation damages wellbeing and collaboration because
bullets: office workers build friendships and team spirit more easily
face-to-face collaboration is often more effective
loneliness and disconnection can affect mental health

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: In conclusion, working from home offers significant advantages but also genuine drawbacks.
sections.idea: The benefits clearly outweigh the costs when
bullets: employers make deliberate efforts to maintain social connection
a hybrid approach combines flexibility with regular office time

fashion-industry.label: Fashion Industry
fashion-industry.question: Does the fashion industry have a bad effect on people's lives?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: The fashion industry has enormous influence over how people look, spend, and feel about themselves.
sections.idea: I believe it creates serious problems in three key areas:
bullets: the pressure on appearance and self-image
the high cost of fashionable clothing
the devastating environmental impact of fast fashion

sections.title: Appearance Pressure
sections.phrase: One damaging effect of the fashion industry is the pressure it places on people's appearance.
sections.idea: Advertisements promote unrealistic beauty standards that
bullets: cause teenagers to feel inadequate and self-conscious
damage self-esteem when trends cannot be followed
create a culture of constant comparison and anxiety

sections.title: Expensive Clothing
sections.phrase: Fashionable clothing is also extremely expensive, creating financial stress for many families.
sections.idea: Designer brands charge premium prices and
bullets: create social pressure to buy branded products
make teenagers feel excluded if they cannot afford trends
encourage wasteful, unnecessary spending throughout the year

sections.title: Environmental Impact
sections.phrase: Perhaps most seriously, fast fashion causes significant damage to the environment.
sections.idea: Cheap, disposable clothing creates enormous waste —
bullets: millions of tonnes end up in landfills annually
production pollutes rivers and consumes vast amounts of water
the environmental cost is both devastating and largely invisible

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: In conclusion, the fashion industry has several genuinely damaging effects on people's lives.
sections.idea: It creates social pressure, encourages overspending, and
bullets: harms the environment in ways that affect everyone
urgently needs to be regulated and reformed

learning-languages.label: Learning Languages
learning-languages.question: Are there more reasons to learn a foreign language than just passing a test?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Learning a foreign language has many valuable benefits that go far beyond passing an examination.
sections.idea: I will argue there are many meaningful reasons throughout life, including
bullets: the genuine pleasure language learning brings
the personal challenge and confidence it builds
the significant career opportunities it creates

sections.title: Pleasure
sections.phrase: Learning a new language brings genuine intellectual pleasure and personal satisfaction.
sections.idea: Discovering new words and structures is stimulating because
bullets: speaking while travelling creates memorable and rewarding experiences
understanding another culture becomes possible through its language
the learning journey itself is intrinsically enjoyable

sections.title: Personal Challenge
sections.phrase: Language learning also represents a deeply rewarding personal challenge.
sections.idea: Mastering a new skill builds real confidence —
bullets: completing a course creates lasting pride and a sense of achievement
motivation to improve and progress grows steadily over time
the challenge itself develops mental discipline and resilience

sections.title: Career Opportunities
sections.phrase: Furthermore, speaking a foreign language creates excellent career opportunities.
sections.idea: Companies actively need multilingual employees, which means
bullets: bilingual candidates stand out significantly in the job market
language skills lead to better positions and higher salaries
they open doors in international business, diplomacy, and tourism

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: In conclusion, there are many compelling reasons to learn a language beyond passing a test.
sections.idea: Language learning provides pleasure, builds personal confidence, and
bullets: creates real and lasting career advantages
enriches life in ways a test result never can

learning-history.label: Learning History
learning-history.question: Should everyone be taught the history of their own country?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: History is a vital subject that helps us understand who we are and how the world came to be.
sections.idea: I strongly believe everyone should study their country's history because it
bullets: teaches invaluable lessons from the past
provides essential context for present-day events
strengthens national identity and community

sections.title: Learning from the Past
sections.phrase: One of the strongest arguments for history education is that it teaches lessons from the past.
sections.idea: Understanding past events helps us avoid repeating mistakes —
bullets: learning about wars teaches the vital importance of peace
historical injustices remind us to protect human rights today
informed citizens make better decisions for the future

sections.title: Understanding the Present
sections.phrase: History also provides essential context for understanding current events and politics.
sections.idea: Focusing only on the future is insufficient because
bullets: political and social situations are clearer with historical background
better-informed choices can be made when context is understood
today's conflicts and alliances are rooted in historical events

sections.title: National Identity
sections.phrase: Finally, national history strengthens a sense of shared identity and community pride.
sections.idea: Understanding our collective past creates connection because
bullets: knowing significant events builds national unity
cultural heritage becomes meaningful and worth preserving
a shared story binds communities together across generations

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: In conclusion, national history education is absolutely essential in every school curriculum.
sections.idea: It provides vital lessons, contextualises the present, and
bullets: strengthens both individual identity and national community
prepares young people to be informed and responsible citizens

sports-money.label: Sports Stars' Pay
sports-money.question: Are famous sports people paid too much money?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Famous athletes earn extraordinarily high salaries, raising the question of whether such payments are justified.
sections.idea: This essay will argue that while athletes provide entertainment and work hard,
bullets: sponsorship deals push earnings to excessive levels
the contrast with other professions is increasingly difficult to justify
public debate on this issue is entirely understandable

sections.title: Entertainment Value
sections.phrase: Athletes certainly deserve payment for the entertainment they provide to millions.
sections.idea: Major sporting events attract enormous global audiences because
bullets: they are watched by billions around the world
athletes have become highly valuable entertainers
broadcasters and sponsors pay enormous sums for this audience

sections.title: Hard Work
sections.phrase: It is also true that athletes work incredibly hard to reach the top level.
sections.idea: Years of intensive training and personal sacrifice demonstrate
bullets: the extraordinary dedication required to compete at elite level
that Olympic athletes train for hours every single day
a level of commitment that deserves genuine recognition

sections.title: Excessive Sponsorship
sections.phrase: However, sponsorship and endorsement deals push earnings to unreasonable levels.
sections.idea: Earning millions for appearing in advertisements inflates incomes so that
bullets: some athletes earn more from endorsements than actual sport
the gap with nurses, teachers, and firefighters becomes indefensible
the system rewards fame and marketability over genuine contribution

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: In conclusion, while athletes work hard and entertain millions, many are significantly overpaid.
sections.idea: Excessive sponsorship income pushes their earnings to levels that
bullets: bear no relation to the value they add to society
are difficult to defend when compared to other vital professions

self-employment.label: Self-Employment
self-employment.question: Is it better to be self-employed or to work for somebody else?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Self-employment has become increasingly popular, but it comes with both advantages and disadvantages.
sections.idea: This essay will analyse both options by examining
bullets: the independence and freedom of working for yourself
the financial security that traditional employment provides
the very different work-life balance each option offers

sections.title: Independence
sections.phrase: The main advantage of self-employment is the independence and freedom it provides.
sections.idea: Choosing your own hours, projects, and clients means
bullets: workers feel more motivated and personally satisfied
freelancers work when they are most productive
career direction is shaped entirely by personal choice

sections.title: Financial Security
sections.phrase: However, a key disadvantage of self-employment is its unpredictable and unstable income.
sections.idea: Without a guaranteed salary or employee benefits,
bullets: financial planning becomes significantly more difficult
irregular income creates stress throughout the year
there is no protection from sick pay or redundancy

sections.title: Work-Life Balance
sections.phrase: Work-life balance also differs significantly between the two options.
sections.idea: Self-employed workers often struggle to separate professional and personal life because
bullets: there are no clear boundaries between work time and free time
they may feel pressured to work constantly to maintain income
holidays and rest can feel impossible to justify

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: To sum up, both options have genuine merits and real challenges.
sections.idea: The best choice ultimately depends on
bullets: a person's financial situation, risk tolerance, and priorities
whether they value freedom or stability more in their working life

advertising-society.label: Advertising
advertising-society.question: Is advertising a very good thing for society?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Advertising is everywhere in modern society and has significant effects on how people think and spend.
sections.idea: While it has some benefits, I believe advertising causes more harm than good —
bullets: it keeps consumers informed about products and deals
it creates healthy competition between businesses
but it also manipulates consumers through psychological techniques

sections.title: Keeping Informed
sections.phrase: Advertising does help consumers stay informed about new products and services.
sections.idea: Companies communicate new offerings and deals, helping people to
bullets: discover useful innovations and relevant discounts
make better-informed shopping decisions
compare products and find the best value available

sections.title: Competition
sections.phrase: It also encourages healthy competition between businesses, which benefits consumers.
sections.idea: When companies compete to attract customers, they
bullets: improve the quality of their products and services
reduce prices to remain competitive in the market
invest in innovation to stay ahead of rivals

sections.title: Manipulation
sections.phrase: However, advertising frequently manipulates consumers through sophisticated psychological techniques.
sections.idea: It creates artificial needs and desires, encouraging people to
bullets: buy unnecessary products they would not otherwise want
spend beyond their means chasing manufactured aspirations
feel inadequate without owning the latest advertised goods

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: In conclusion, advertising has both positive and negative aspects.
sections.idea: However, its manipulation of consumers significantly outweighs its informational benefits, and
bullets: stricter regulation of misleading advertising is urgently needed
critical media literacy should be taught to young people

helping-others.label: Helping Others
helping-others.question: Should everyone regularly do something to help other people?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Helping others is a fundamental part of what makes communities function and individuals flourish.
sections.idea: I strongly believe everyone should contribute regularly because it strengthens
bullets: personal relationships with friends and family
the wider community and society
the individual's own personal development and wellbeing

sections.title: Friends and Family
sections.phrase: Helping the people close to us significantly strengthens personal relationships.
sections.idea: Supporting others builds deep trust and connection because
bullets: helping during difficulties brings people emotionally closer
relationships become more resilient and meaningful over time
mutual support creates the foundation of lasting friendship

sections.title: Society
sections.phrase: Contributing to society is also essential for the wellbeing of entire communities.
sections.idea: Communities function better when people support each other —
bullets: volunteering directly improves local services and spaces
social trust and cooperation increase noticeably
society as a whole becomes stronger and more cohesive

sections.title: Personal Development
sections.phrase: Furthermore, helping others has a genuinely positive effect on personal development.
sections.idea: Giving builds empathy, confidence, and valuable life skills because
bullets: the act of helping increases personal happiness and life satisfaction
both the helper and the recipient benefit from every act of generosity
young people who volunteer develop stronger character and social awareness

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: In conclusion, everyone should make helping others a regular part of their lives.
sections.idea: It strengthens relationships, improves communities, and
bullets: promotes personal growth in ways few other activities can
creates the kind of society in which everyone benefits

online-learning.label: Online Learning
online-learning.question: Is online learning better than traditional classroom learning?

sections.title: Introduction
sections.phrase: Online learning has become increasingly widespread, raising questions about its effectiveness compared to traditional schooling.
sections.idea: This essay will analyse both methods by considering
bullets: the flexibility and accessibility of online education
the lack of social interaction and face-to-face support
the self-discipline required to succeed independently

sections.title: Flexibility
sections.phrase: The main advantage of online learning is its exceptional flexibility.
sections.idea: Students can study anywhere with an internet connection, meaning
bullets: recorded lessons can be accessed at convenient times
education becomes accessible to those in remote areas
learners can progress at their own pace and revisit material

sections.title: Interaction
sections.phrase: However, a key disadvantage is the limited social contact that online learning provides.
sections.idea: Students miss out on discussions, friendships, and immediate feedback —
bullets: learning can feel isolating and demotivating over time
classrooms provide irreplaceable face-to-face interaction
peer learning and spontaneous collaboration are impossible online

sections.title: Self-Discipline
sections.phrase: Online learning also requires a high level of self-discipline that many students lack.
sections.idea: Without direct supervision, students must organise themselves and
bullets: many struggle significantly with procrastination at home
studying alone without structure is genuinely challenging
motivation can collapse without the social environment of a classroom

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.phrase: In conclusion, both online and classroom learning have valuable advantages.
sections.idea: Combining the flexibility of online learning with
bullets: the interaction and support of the traditional classroom
would represent the most effective and balanced approach to education
